description_long: This object is a vintage portable computer, reminiscent of early 1980s luggable models such as the Osborne 1 or Compaq Portable. It features a boxy, beige plastic chassis with a built-in monochrome CRT display set into the upper half of the front face. Below the screen is a full-sized mechanical keyboard, which appears detachable and is connected by a coiled cable. The rear of the unit has a prominent carrying handle and multiple ventilation slats, as well as several ports and switches for connectivity and power. The sides are solid with additional ventilation grilles, and the overall design is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements. This type of computer would typically be used in urban environments for business or field work, and its robust construction suggests it was designed for portability and durability.
description: A vintage, boxy portable computer with a built-in CRT screen, detachable keyboard, and a sturdy beige plastic case featuring ventilation slats and a carrying handle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the CRT screen, full keyboard, and coiled cable connection.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the thickness of the chassis and vertical ventilation grilles.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the carrying handle, extensive ventilation slats, and multiple ports and switches.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the solid side panel and the profile of the keyboard and screen.
attribute_screen_state: The screen is open and turned on, displaying visible content with text or code.
attribute_keyboard_layout: QWERTY layout with a full function row and numeric keypad, standard for English-language computers.
